title: Davilanthus hidalgoanus
intro: Davilanthus hidalgoanus, also known as the Mexican damiana, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The health benefits of Davilanthus hidalgoanus include its potential to aid in anxiety and stress relief, improve sleep quality, and exhibit aphrodisiac properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Davilanthus hidalgoanus is commonly prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to support sexual health, alleviate menstrual cramps, and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Davilanthus hidalgoanus is a plant species, and it belongs to the family Celastraceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the staff vine family. Within the Celastraceae family, Davilanthus hidalgoanus is classified under the genus Davilanthus. The taxonomy of this plant is further supported by its classification as a member of the order Celastrales. Additionally, it is also classified as a member of the subclass Rosidae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other species.
